lesson.claim: Project Telos has a twelfth-wave PDE proof-packet ladder package that adds a bounded Navier-Stokes periodic skew-symmetry packet, records BuildLang/buildc scientific-runtime heat-equation receipt evidence, routes the work through Forum/Index, demotes arXiv metadata to source leads, creates outreach and whitepaper drafts, and keeps Navier-Stokes parent-problem claims UNVERIFIABLE.
lesson.scope: Local twelfth-wave documentation and receipt package only. This is not a proof of Navier-Stokes existence and smoothness, not theorem-prover replay, not physical-fluid validation, not source-body review of arXiv papers, not warning-clean buildc certification, and not an accepted publication.

scaffold.prompt: Restate, in your own words, exactly what this claim asserts and nothing more: "Project Telos has a twelfth-wave PDE proof-packet ladder package that adds a bounded Navier-Stokes periodic skew-symmetry packet, records BuildLang/buildc scientific-runtime heat-equation receipt evidence, routes the work through Forum/Index, demotes arXiv metadata to source leads, creates outreach and whitepaper drafts, and keeps Navier-Stokes parent-problem claims UNVERIFIABLE." (scope: Local twelfth-wave documentation and receipt package only. This is not a proof of Navier-Stokes existence and smoothness, not theorem-prover replay, not physical-fluid validation, not source-body review of arXiv papers, not warning-clean buildc certification, and not an accepted publication.).

Judging by their refs alone (docs/outreach/TWELFTH-WAVE-PDE-PROOF-LADDER-2026-07-02.md, docs/research/whitepapers/PDE-PROOF-PACKET-LADDER-2026-07-02.md, docs/outreach/TWELFTH-WAVE-CONTENT-QUEUE-2026-07-02.md, docs/outreach/receipts/twelfth-wave-source-route-summary-2026-07-02.json, docs/outreach/receipts/twelfth-wave-tooling-run-2026-07-02.json, docs/outreach/receipts/twelfth-wave/buildlang-heat-equation-energy.receipt.json, docs/research/proof-packets/navier-stokes-periodic-skew-symmetry-v0/crucible-run.json), predict what each recorded source could contribute as evidence for or against the claim.
The packet's overall verdict is MATCH. Before reading anything else, derive what a MATCH verdict must mean about the relationship between the claim and the recorded checks.
List the checks you would run to reach your own verdict on this claim, then compare your list against what the packet's verdict implies was actually checked.

retrievalQuestions.question: What evidence would falsify this claim: "Project Telos has a twelfth-wave PDE proof-packet ladder package that adds a bounded Navier-Stokes periodic skew-symmetry packet, records BuildLang/buildc scientific-runtime heat-equation receipt evidence, routes the work through Forum/Index, demotes arXiv metadata to source leads, creates outreach and whitepaper drafts, and keeps Navier-Stokes parent-problem claims UNVERIFIABLE."?
Which recorded source would you re-check first, and what does its content hash let you prove about it?
What does an overall verdict of MATCH entitle you to claim, and what does it not?

The packet's scope is "Local twelfth-wave documentation and receipt package only. This is not a proof of Navier-Stokes existence and smoothness, not theorem-prover replay, not physical-fluid validation, not source-body review of arXiv papers, not warning-clean buildc certification, and not an accepted publication.". Give one claim that would exceed that scope.
